description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring a set of dark, rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a low, rectangular base. The panels are arranged in a slightly tilted configuration, likely to optimize sunlight capture, and are supported by a robust frame structure. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and possibly concrete, providing stability and durability. There are colored elements on the base, including red and orange sections, which may indicate electrical components or safety markings. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed panel surfaces and a minimalistic mounting system. No visible cabling or inverter housing is apparent from the exterior views.
description: A compact solar panel array mounted on a sturdy rectangular base with colored accents and a slightly tilted panel configuration.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main solar panels and colored base elements, with the panels' tilt clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled orientation of the panels and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the panel array and the colored sections on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel tilt and the arrangement of colored components on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and a solid base
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 panels mounted side by side on a rectangular base
